---
title: "a"
output: html_document
date: "2025-12-07"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

df1 <- read_csv("game_results/gomoku_AB_d2_vs_AB_d3_1.csv")
df2 <- read_csv("game_results/gomoku_AB_d2_vs_AB_d3_2.csv")

games <- bind_rows(df1, df2)
```

```{r}
light_orange <- "#eed5b1"
gray_brown   <- "#8C6D62"

ggplot(overall_win, aes(x = winner, y = win_rate, fill = winner)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(win_rate, accuracy = 0.1)),
            vjust = -0.3,
            size = 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(light_orange, gray_brown)) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Overall Win Rate: AB Depth 2 vs Depth 3",
    x = "Winner",
    y = "Win Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )


```
